description:
You are a capacity-planner who has finished collecting resource-usage data and now need to store it securely.  
Inside the directory  
/home/user/capacity_tasks  
there is a plain-text file named  

/home/user/capacity_tasks/resource_usage.csv  

Your goal is to:

1. Encrypt that CSV file with GnuPG using symmetric (passphrase-based) encryption.  
   • Cipher algorithm must be AES256 (the default for modern GPG, so you do not have to override it explicitly).  
   • The passphrase to use is exactly:  
     Capacity#2024  
   • The resulting encrypted file must be created in the **same** directory and must be named  
     /home/user/capacity_tasks/resource_usage.csv.gpg  

2. Immediately after a successful encryption, create a tiny log file called  

/home/user/capacity_tasks/gpg_result.log  

   The log file **must contain exactly two lines, no extra blank lines or spaces**:

   ENCRYPTION_OK  
   FILENAME: resource_usage.csv.gpg  

   (Line-1 is the literal word “ENCRYPTION_OK”; line-2 starts with “FILENAME:” followed by a single space and the filename.)

Verification notes for you:
• Only the encrypted “.gpg” file and the log file should be new; do not rename or delete the original CSV.  
• Use one straightforward GPG command; no additional tooling is required.  
• The automated checker will decrypt the “.gpg” file with the same passphrase and compare its content to the original CSV, and it will read the log file to ensure the two lines are present exactly as shown above.
truth:
Pre-existing file (before the agent starts):

Path: /home/user/capacity_tasks/resource_usage.csv  
Content (four lines, newline at the end of each line):
timestamp,cpu_usage_percent,memory_usage_mb
2024-06-01T00:00:00Z,32,2048
2024-06-01T01:00:00Z,45,3072
2024-06-01T02:00:00Z,29,1984

Expected artifacts after the agent completes:

1. Encrypted file  
   Path: /home/user/capacity_tasks/resource_usage.csv.gpg  
   • Must be a valid GPG symmetric-cipher file decryptable with passphrase “Capacity#2024”.  
   • When decrypted, byte-for-byte content must equal the original CSV above.

2. Log file  
   Path: /home/user/capacity_tasks/gpg_result.log  
   Exact content (including newline after each line, UNIX line endings):
ENCRYPTION_OK
FILENAME: resource_usage.csv.gpg

No extra whitespace before/after either line.

Permissions: all files under /home/user/capacity_tasks should remain user-writable (default 0644 is acceptable).

The testing harness will:
• Check that /home/user/capacity_tasks/resource_usage.csv still exists unchanged.  
• Confirm /home/user/capacity_tasks/resource_usage.csv.gpg exists and decrypts correctly with the given passphrase.  
• Read /home/user/capacity_tasks/gpg_result.log and verify the exact two-line format shown above.
name: task_000000_538633f9
